description: Detects memory mismanagement in C++: delete-then-use (use-after-free), dangling string_view from temporaries, free() on new-allocated memory. Simplified from Rust per D-07: flags delete_expression nodes broadly (cannot track cross-statement variable liveness or verify string_view initializer type without full data-flow analysis).

flag.message: delete expression detected -- verify pointer is not used after delete (use-after-free), delete[] is matched to new[], and string_view is not initialized from a temporary (dangling reference). Consider using smart pointers to eliminate manual delete.
